def: s.m. The fourth Hindū month, July-August (beginning when the sun enters Cancer: its full moon is near Śravaṇā or α Aquilæ)
more: n. m. S. श्रावण The 4th Hin. month, the full moon of which is near श्रावण or Aquila; July—August. 1. Barse sāvan, to hoṅ pāṅch, ke bāvan . Prov. Pour July rains! of five make fifty two! (A good crop.) 2. Sāvan hare, na Bhādoṅ sūkhe . Prov. Nor July green, nor August dry (Uniform prosperity). 3. Āge nāī aur Bāman, pīchhe māī-jāyā bīran, Sāvan āiyāṅ, Nāī ke ko kartī, 'Rām, Rām' māī-jāë ko gale selagāë Bāman ko detī mūṅdhlā, māī-jāë ko takhat bichhāë Wom. song. Jhūlnā. The barber and the Brahman. come, my brother next behind I meet, July hath come! the first 'Ram Ram,' the next with fond embrace I greet, A moṛah seat to Brahman given, a board spread for my brother meet. sāvan bhādoṅ , n. m. 1. Sun and rain; jackals' wedding. 2. A kind of lattice work. 3. A kind of firework. sāvan bhādoṅ milnā , v. n. To rain on the day on which Sāvan ends and ushers in Bhādoṅ. sāvan kī jhaṛī , Constant show…

s. m. The name of the fourth Hindū month, the full moon of which is near श्रवणा or α Aquilæ, July-August. ساون هري نه بهادون سوکهي sāwan hare na bhādoṅ sūkhe (Flourishing in summer and not fading in autumn) is applied to express continuance in the same state or situation.
subs: s.m. Sunshine and rain; — a kind of lattice-work; — a kind of firework
s.f. The constant showers of Sāwan
'Flourishing in summer and not fading in autumn'; continuing in the same state or condition.
wety: Inherited from Middle Hindi ساون (savn /⁠sāvan⁠/) (c. 1780), from Prakrit 𑀲𑀸𑀯𑀡 (sāvaṇa), from Sanskrit श्रावण (śrā́vaṇa). Cognate with Bengali শাওন (śaōn), Gujarati સાવણ (sāvaṇ), Punjabi ਸਉਣ (sauṇa) / ساون (sāvn), Sindhi سانوڻ (sānoṇ) / सांवणु (sā̃vaṇu). Compare Pashto ساوڼ (sāwaṇ).

def: s.m. Institutor of a sacrifice or employer of priests at a sacrifice; the conclusion of a sacrifice or the ceremonies by which it is terminated; — a name of Varuṇa; — a month of thirty solar days; a natural or civil day (from sunrise to sunset); a day reckoned from sunrise to sunrise (of which 1,577,917,828 are counted in a yuga or cycle of 4,320,000 years).
wety: Inherited from Middle Hindi ساون (savn /⁠sāvan⁠/) (c. 1780), from Prakrit 𑀲𑀸𑀯𑀡 (sāvaṇa), from Sanskrit श्रावण (śrā́vaṇa). Cognate with Bengali শাওন (śaōn), Gujarati સાવણ (sāvaṇ), Punjabi ਸਉਣ (sauṇa) / ساون (sāvn), Sindhi سانوڻ (sānoṇ) / सांवणु (sā̃vaṇu). Compare Pashto ساوڼ (sāwaṇ).

def: s.m. The act of bearing children, bringing forth young, generation; the moon; — the act of extracting or pouring out the juice of the Soma plant; a libation; a sacrifice; drinking the soma juice; bathing, ablution (purificatory or expiatory).

s.f. The day of full moon in the month Sāwan; the harvest of Sāwan, the ḵẖarīf or autumn harvest; the rejoicings of the month of Sāwan (in which the scattered members of a family assemble under the paternal roof, and presents are interchanged between families where an engagement is pending); sweetmeats and fruits, &c. presented by the bridegroom (or by the parents) to the bride in Sāwan; the ceremony of presenting a fiancée with the materials for a swing (as coloured poles, ropes, &c.) in the month Sāwan; the materials for a swing which are presented to a fiancée in Sāwan.
more: n. f. 1. The kharīf or autumn harvest. 2. The full moon in the month of Sāvan . 3. Sweetmeats and fruits, etc. presented by the bridegroom to the bride in Sāvan .

def: s.m.f. (dialec.), Giver of a good omen.
adj. & s.f. pl. of s̤āniya, q.v.
s.f. corr. of sawārī, q.v. s.f. Riding; horsemanship; anything on which one rides, vehicle (beast, horse, &c.), conveyance; equipage, cavalcade, suite; — a trick in wrestling
subs: s.f. A cess to defray the expense of entertaining some person of rank, or one in authority
(-ko), To give (one) a ride or a mount; to carry, &c.

def: s.m. A news-writer, an intelligencer (an officer stationed by the Mog̠al government in distant provinces to transmit weekly to the court an account of all public transactions, such as the collection of revenue, the management of lands, and the state of the country)
more: n. pl. of سانحه from سنح to be manifest. Occurrences; incidents; events. savāneh-nigār, vaqāë-nigār , n. m. A newswriter. An officer stationed by the Mogul Government in distant provinces to transmit weekly to the court an account of all public transactions, such as the collection of revenue, the management of lands, and the state of the country. savānah-i-umrī . The incidents of one's life.
s. plur. (of سنوح from سنح To be manifest) Accidents, occurrences, incidents, events. سوانح نگار sawāniḥ-nigār, A news-writer, intelligencer.

def: corr. of sawār, q.v. 1˚ = sābar or sābal; — 2˚ = sāwal or sāʼūl, qq.v. s.m. A large species of stag, a kind of elk; — leather of elk's (or any kind of deer's) hide, chamois, wash-leather.
v.t. = saṅwārnā, q.v. v.t. To prepare, to construct; to dress, decorate, adorn, deck; to arrange, methodize, regulate, rectify, correct, mend, improve.
adj. & s.m. Brave, warlike, heroic; — a leader, general; warrior, hero, champion.
more: adj. S. सामन्त Brave; valiant ( bīr , 1.). sāv'ant , n. m. A warrior ( bīr , 1).
adj. Brave, heroick. s. m. A hero.

def: s.f. Bravery, valour, heroism.
shower (device for bathing)
wety: Borrowed from English shower.

def: s.f. Lying-in chamber; impurity from childbirth (cf. the forms soṛ, sowaṛ, and sauṅr).
s.m. = شور śavar, q.v. 1˚ = sābar or sābal; — 2˚ = sāwal or sāʼūl, qq.v. s.m. A large species of stag, a kind of elk; — leather of elk's (or any kind of deer's) hide, chamois, wash-leather.
more: n. m. S. शूकर. 1. A hog. Tulsī! as bālak soṅ nahīṅ neh, kaha jap, jog, samād kiye? Nar we khar, shūkar, swān samān, kaho jag meṅ phal kaun jīye? Tulsi! the man who Ram loves not, nor dwells upon, nor worships him, Is as an ass, or hog, or dog, what profit is his life to him? 2. A term of abuse. 3. ( Contemp .) ( sūar-sain ) A greedy, filthy, or miserly person. sūar kā khubhār , E. A pigsty. sūar kā gosht , n. m. Pork; ham; bacon. sūar kī charbī , n. f. Hogs' lard. sūar kī musāṅḍ . A desperate onset or attack. sūar gheṅṭā ; E. sūar kuttī , n. m. A pig. sūr'ī, sūarnī , n. f. 1. A sow. 2. ( Contemp .) A mother of many children.

def: s.m. A barbarian, a savage; a member of a particular tribe of barbarians who inhabit the mountainous districts of India, and wear peacock's feathers as decoration; — name of Śivan-water.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian شور (šōr).

def: 1˚ = sābar or sābal; — 2˚ = sāwal or sāʼūl, qq.v. s.m. A large species of stag, a kind of elk; — leather of elk's (or any kind of deer's) hide, chamois, wash-leather.
more: n. 1. An implement for picking out bricks or house breaking; a crowbar. 2. A centre bit.
n. f. S. सु to beget. 1. See jachchākhānā . 2. Impurity or uncleanness incident to childbirth. Inkā pānī mat pīnā, īnke hāṅ sāvaṛ hai .

def: s.m. = sawar or śavar, q.v. 1˚ = sābar or sābal; — 2˚ = sāwal or sāʼūl, qq.v. s.m. A large species of stag, a kind of elk; — leather of elk's (or any kind of deer's) hide, chamois, wash-leather.

ety: sawār, q.v.+ī = Zend aff. i = S. इ; cf. aswārī
def: s.f. Riding; horsemanship; anything on which one rides, vehicle (beast, horse, &c.), conveyance; equipage, cavalcade, suite; — a trick in wrestling
more: n. f. 1. See asvārī . 2. A trick in wrestling. 1. Ghoṛe kī savārī, chaltā janāzā . Prov. Mount a horse, count on a corpse. 2. Are bhāī! kisāṅṛ ke to pāoṅ hī suārī saëṅ, suārī kī kimaiṅ loṛ nā . W. Rus. Why man! the peasant's legs are his horse, he does not want a mount.
s. f. 1. Riding. 2. Equipage, cavalcade, suite.
subs: s.f. A cess to defray the expense of entertaining some person of rank, or one in authority
(-ko), To give (one) a ride or a mount; to carry, &c.

def: s.f. (dialec.) = śambālī, q.v.
adj. (f. -ā), Of the same colour; of like appearance, like, resembling; of the same kind, or tribe, or class, homogeneous.
more: n. m. An edible tuberous root; Arum campanulatum . Roxb.
n. m. S. स्वर्ण Gold. Are yoh aukhad soran-kāyā kar de sai . Rus. Why! this medicine makes your body shine like gold! soran kī kāyā , n. A healthy, robust person.

def: v.t. = saṅwārnā, q.v. v.t. To prepare, to construct; to dress, decorate, adorn, deck; to arrange, methodize, regulate, rectify, correct, mend, improve.
s.f. An epithet of Ćhāyā, wife of the sun.
more: v. a. To flog; lash; whip.

def: s.m. Sameness or similiarity of colour; identity of caste or class.
adj. (Braj.) = sāṅwlā, q.v. adj. (f. -ī), Of a dark or sallow complexion; dark, swarthy; sallow, brown, nut-brown; of handsome countenance; an epithet of Krishṇ
subs: adj. (f. -ī), Brown and salty; rich brown; — piquant, pleasing
adj. & s.m. Having a sallow or a handsome countenance; — one who has a sallow countenance, &c., an epithet of Krishṇ.

def: s.f. A young woman.
s.f. = saha-vasti, q.v.
s.f. = saha-vasti, q.v.
adj. (f. -ā), Together with a calf, having a calf; having offspring or descendants.

s.m. = sawaiyā, q.v. adj. With a quarter more, &c. ( = sawāyā, q.v.); — s.m. A quarter more; a weight of 1 ¼ ser; the multiplication table of 1 ¼ times; a kind of verse or measure (consisting of four lines, each of 16+15 = 31 instants, the last foot being a trochee); a song in this measure; a singer of the sawaiyā
subs: s.m. A species of metre ( = sawaiyā).

def: s.m.f. Of the same age; coeval, contemporary; — a contemporary; a friend of the same age.
adj. Composed of parts, possessed of limbs or members.
adj. & adv. Early, timely; in good time, soon.
more: adj. Opp. of aver . Early; timely. Aver saver huā hi kartī hai . Early or late as ruled by fate.

def: s.m. The early morning, dawn; — sawere (obl. form. of sawerā), adv. In the early morning, at dawn, early, soon.
more: n. m. S. सुवेला Morning; dawn. Jahāṅ murgā nahīṅ hotā, kyā vahāṅ saverā nahīṅ hotā? Prov. What! is there no dawn where there is no cock. (I can do without you). saverā h. , v. n. To break (the day); to be dawn ( din nikalnā ).
s. m. Morning: see سبيرا saberā.
wety: Inherited from Sanskrit *सवेल (*savela). Cognate with Bhojpuri सबेर (sabēr).

def: adv. See s.v. sawerā (of which it is the format. sing.). s.m. The early morning, dawn; — sawere (obl. form. of sawerā), adv. In the early morning, at dawn, early, soon.
more: s. m. inflect. of سويرا sawerā, In the morning, early. See سبيري sabere.

def: adv. At morn; early. Savere uṭhne se jī khush rahtū hai .
s.f. corr. of سوغات saug̠āt, q.v.
adj. Having the mystical letter or syllable of a magical formula at its commencement (a mantra, or form of prayer, &c.).
adj. Changed; transformed, transfigured.
adj. (f. -ā), Sportive, playful, wanton, amorous, coquettish.
adj. (f. -ā), Astonished, surprised; doubtful.
adj. contrac. of next, q.v.
s.m. The sun. (This is the name by which the deity is invoked in the Gāyatrī).
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀲𑁄𑀢𑁆𑀢 (sotta), from Sanskrit *स्रोत्र (*srotra), from स्रु (sru, “to flow”).

def: adj. Relating or belonging to the sun; descended from the sun, belonging to the solar dynasty; — s.m. The sun; — a name of Śiva; an epithet of Karṇa, the child of the sun; name of a Vasu; name of one of the Nakshatras or lunar asterisms.

s.f. A beam or ray of light; a cluster of solar rays; name of a celebrated verse of the Rig-veda (regarded as the most sacred verse of the Veda, and called Sāvitrī as addressed to the sun); the ceremony of investiture with the sacred thread; the sacrificial or sacred thread; — an epithet of Umā (wife of Śiva); and of the wife of Satyavān.
adj. Possessing discrimination, &c.
adj. & s.m. (dialec.) Having black ears; — a black-eared creature or animal.
v.t. (rustic) = sewnā, q.v. v.t. To attend on, serve; to adore, worship; to heed, give attention to; to protect, watch over, guard; to brood, incubate, rear, hatch, sit on (eggs)
subs: To sit on eggs, to brood; (fig.) to mope.

def: n. f. ( Agric .) The share of the grain given from the threshing floor to Brahmans, faqīrs, jogīs , &c.
adj. (f. -ā), Left, left-hand; to the left (cf. ćap); south, southern; reverse, contrary, backward.
s.m. = سايه sāya, q.v.
more: s. m. Shade. See سايه sāya.

def: s.m. End, close, termination; close of day, evening; — an arrow
more: n. m. S. छाया 1. See parchhāīṅ . 2. Shelter; protection 3. The influence of an evil spirit. 4. A gown; petticoat.

s. m. 1. Shadow, shade, shelter, protection. 2. An apparition, spectre. 3. A petticoat. سايه بان sāya-bān, See سايبان sāya-bān. سايه تلي آنا sāya tale ānā, To take protection or shelter. سايه کسيکا پڙنا sāya kisī kā paṛnā, To imitate the manner of another. سايه دار sāya-dār, 1. Shady. 2. A protector. سايه گستر sāya-gustar, Spreading a shade, shading; shader, favourer. سايه هونا کسي کو sāya honā kisī ko, A demon appearing to, or possessing any one.
subs: vulg. sāyaṅ-kāl, q.v., s.m. Evening, eventide.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian سَایَه (sāya). Doublet of چھاؤں (chāõ).

def: s.m. Shadow, shade; shelter, protection; apparition, spectre; influence (of an evil spirit); — a petticoat
subs: vulg. sāʼe-bān, s.m. Canopy; sunshade, umbrella, parasol; roof or fly of a tent; thatched roof
or sāya-parwarda, adj. & s.m. 'Brought up in the shade,' delicately nurtured; — a protégé; an inexperienced person; — a slave
(-par), The shadow (of one, -kā) to fall (upon); to come under the influence (of); to imitate
(-ke), or sāye-meṅ ānā (-ke), To come under the shadow or protection (of), to take shelter (with)
vulg. sāʼe-dār, adj. & s.m. Shady, umbrageous; affording shelter or protection; — a protector
(-par), To cast a shadow; to afford shelter or protection (to), to take under (one's) protection, to foster, to show favour (to); — sāya-figan, adj. & s.m. Casting a shadow; — shadow-caster
adj. & s.m. Shade-diffusing, shading, shady; sheltering, protecting; — shelterer, protector, favourer
(-ke), and sāya honā or ho jānā (-kā), To come under the shadow (of), to fall under the influence (of an evil spirit, or a fairy or jinn), to be possessed (by an evil spirit).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian سَایَه (sāya). Doublet of چھاؤں (chāõ).

def: shady; shadow-giving
wety: From سایَہ (sāya, “shadow”) + ـدار (-dār).

def: s.m. See s.v. سايه sāya.
more: (comp. of سايه Shade, and the particle بان Keeper) s. m. A canopy.

def: corr. of shāyad, q.v. adv. It may be, perhaps, perchance, possibly, probably
subs: It may be that it is, it may be so, perhaps it is.

def: s.m. An arrow; a dart; a sword; weapon.
s.m. An arrow; a dart; a sword.
s.m. = شيل śail, q.v. part. & s.m. Asking; — asker, interrogator, querist, questioner; applicant, suitor, petitioner; beggar (P. pl. sāʼilān).
n. m. سال he asked him. 1. A questioner; interrogator; querist. 2. A petitioner; suitor; prosecutor; an applicant. 3. A beggar.
more: 1. (part. act. of سأل Heb. To ask) adj. Asking; s. m. An interrogator, asker, querist, a beggar. 2. (part. act. of سيل) adj. Flowing, fluid.

def: adj. Sleepy; slothful, sluggish; — s.m. A snake; a boa-constrictor; a dog; a jackal.
s.m. Name of a learned Brāhmaṇ and commentator (he is thought to have flourished about A.D. 1370).
s.f. (dialec.) 1˚ = sain, 'sign'; 2˚ = senā; — s.m. (dialec.) = sewan, qq.v.
s.m. The longitude of a planet reckoned from the vernal equinoctial point.
s.m. Sleep, repose (see śayan)
s.m. Lying down, sleeping, reposing; sleep; a bed, couch; copulation
subs: (˚na+ić˚), adj. Desirous of sleep; desirous of copulation.

def: s.m. Sagacity, cleverness, shrewdness, cunning, artfulness; maturity.
adj. (dakh.) See سيانا siyānā.

adj. (f. -ī) & s.m. Grown up, adolescent, of age, mature (in understanding), arrived at puberty or years of discretion; knowinG, sagacious, prudent, clever, wise, intelligent; shrewd, cunning, artful, sly; — a knowing or astute person, a sharp or shrewd man; a wise man; a conjurer, an exorcist; a miser.
more: adj. S. सज्ञान. 1. Clever; wise; sagacious; intelligent. 1. Siyānā kavvā khe khāë Prov. The cunning crow eats dirt. (He is so sharp that he cuts himself). 2. Ek main dekhī aisī nārī, peṭ meṅ lugṛā, naṅgī sārī, Pyās lage jab aisī siyānī, aur ke hāth se pīve pānī . Riddle. Ans. Mashal a torch. A female strange I saw, with rags her stomach stuffed, her body bare, When thirsty she that cunning is she from an other's hands drinkse'er. 3. Siyāne to haiṅ bahut se, sab se siyānā chho, Hīnā dekh ho chaugnā, ṭhāḍe par kam ho . Dohā. Full many knowing ones there are, but none so wise as ire, Upon the weak it falls fourfold, on strong men it's less dire. 2. Knowing; canny. 3. Cunning; crafty; artful; shrewd. 1. Qāzī ke ghar ke chūhe bhī siyāne . Prov. Even the rats in the Qazi's (judge's) house are knowing. 2. Banye se siyānā so divānā . Prov. Who more than Banyā (grocer) knowing is a madman is. 4. …
adj. 1. Cunning, artful, shrewd, sly, knowing, sagacious, clever, prudent. 2. Mature (in understanding), grown up.
adj. used substantively, A cunning man, a conjurer.

def: s.m. = H سيانپت सियानपत siyān-pat, स्यानपत syān-pat, s.f. = syānpan, q.v.
bedroom; dormitory; bedchamber
wety: From Sanskrit शयन (śayana, “bed”) + कक्ष (kakṣa, “chamber”).

def: s.m. See s.v. sāya, 'end.'
s.f. corr. of ساعت sāʻat, q.v. s.f. Time; at hour; a short time, a little while; a minute; a moment; — a clock, a watch; — sāʻat-sāz, s.m. Watch-maker ( = ghaṛī sāz).
adj. & s.m. Possessing or enjoying the season of youth; — one who is experiencing the time or the condition of youth.
s.m. corr. of shāh-zāda, q.v.s.v. shāh.
s.m. Union, identification, intimate union, absorption (esp. the ultimate re-absorption of the soul into the divine essence); similarity, likeness.
s.f. = śayyā; and sej, qq.v.
s.f. A bed, couch, sofa (see sej).
oblique/vocative singular; direct plural
oblique plural of سَیَّارَہ (sayyāra, “planet”)

ety: act. part of sāḵẖtan, rt. Zend śać = S. शक्
def: part. & s.m. Making, preparing, effecting; feigning; — maker; counterfeiter (used as last member of compounds; e.g. zinda-sāz, 'making alive,' 'resuscitating'; — ghaṛī-sāz, 'watch-maker'); — s.m. Arms, accoutrements; apparatus; instrument, implement; harness; furniture; ornament; concord, harmony; a musical instrument
more: part. act. ساختن to make. ( In comp .) Making; made; maker; as ghaṛī-sāz a watch-maker.

n. m. 1. Apparatus. 2. Harness. 3. Musical instruments. 4. Ornaments. 5. Concord; harmony. sāz-bāz , n. See sāz , 1, 4, 5. and sāzish . sāz-gār , adj. 1. Fitting; jumping with; agreeing. Yeh ghoṛā hameṅ sāz-gār nahīṅ hai . 2. Auspicious; favorable. sāz o sāmān , n. m. See اسباب 2-4. be-sāz o sāmān, be sar o sāmān , adj. Ped. Unprovided; unfurnished; without the necessary appliances.

1. part. act. (from ساختن) Making, preparing, maker. 2. s. m. Arms, apparatus, accoutrements, harness, furniture. 3. A musical instrument. 4. Concord. ساز باز sāz-bāz, 1. adj. Trim, dressy. 2. s. m. Ornament, apparatus, accoutrement, &c. (the word باز being expletive). سازکار sāz-kār, adj. Proper, consonant, concordant. سازيراق sāz-yarāḳ, s. m. Warlike instruments, furniture, baggage.
subs: adj. Trim, dressy; — s.m. Apparatus, &c.; ornament; concord ( = sāz)
or sāz-gār, adj. Consonant, concordant, in accordance (with); proper, agreeing (with), harmonizing, suiting, befitting
or sāz-gārī, s.f. Concord, harmony, agreement; peace
v.t. To prepare, get ready (necessaries, &c. for); to put in order, to arrange; to cause to accord; to tune (an instrument)
s.m. Apparatus, instruments, implements; appliances; materials; furniture; necessaries, supplies
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian سَاز (sāz).

def: s.m. (local), corr. of sājhā, q.v. s.m. Association, company (in trade), partnership; relation, connection; — share (in a company); — adj. Joint
subs: (-kā), Partnership to be dissolved; connection to be severed; — sājhā ćhūṭnā, Dissolution of partnership
v.n. To form an association or a company; to enter into partnership
or sājhe-meṅ, adv. In partnership, jointly
adv. As a partner or sharer in, with the eye of a partner, as if in co-parcenary.

ety: rt. Zend śać; cf. Pehl. śajak
def: s.f. (orig.) 'Worthy, deserving'; correction, chastisement, nunishment; penalty, retribution
more: n. f. sāz fit. 1. Punishment; chastisement; correction. 2. Requital; penalty badlā , 3.). sazā pānā, yā milnā , v. n. To be punished. sazā par sazā, sazā-i-mutٴaddad , Cumulative punishment. sazā denā , v. a. To award or inflict punishment ( pīḷnā , 3.). sazā se bachne ke vāste . To avoid punishment. sazā taë k., yā bhugatnā , v. a. To complete one's term of punishment. sazā karānā, yā dilānā , v. a. 1. To cause to be punished. 2. To bring to punishment. sazā-i-badanī, yā jismānī , G. G.; H. deh ḍanḍ , n. f. Corporal punishment; flogging; flagellation. sazā-i-phāṅsī, yā maut , n. f. Hanging. sazā-i-phāṅsī kā sazā-vār . G. G. def: adj. & s.m. (f. -ikā), Serving; servile; dependent; — a servant, attendant; a worshipper, a votary; a disciple; follower; dependent; a loyal person, subject
more: n. m. S. सेवक a servant. 1. A servant; an attendant. 2. A worshipper; a votary. 3. A disciple; follower. Sevak soī jāniye rahe bipat meṅ saṅg, Tan-chhāyā jiyūṅ dhūp meṅ rahe sāth ek saṅg . Brand satsaī Disciple true is he who shares your misery. As doth his shade to sunlight cleave continually. sevak-patr , m. A deed or bond by which a person binds himself to servitude either for a term or for life.
s. m. A servant, a worshipper, a votary.
subs: s.m. A deed or bond by which a person binds himself to servitude (either for a term or for life).

def: s. f. Service. See سيوا sewā.

s.f. Service, &c. ( = sevā, q.v.); discipleship; pupilage. s.f. Service, servitude, tending, attendance (on, -kī); employment, situation, office, post, province; honouring, adoration, worship, homage; piety; devotion, addiction (to, -kī); use, practice; resorting (to), frequentation
subs: (-kī), To serve; to attend (on), wait (on); to worship, &c.

ety: sevak+S. इनी; and ई, or इका
def: s.f. = sevikā, q.v. s.f. A female servant; disciple; votary, worshipper.

ety: sevak+S. इनी; and ई, or इका
def: s.f. = sevikā, q.v. s.f. A female servant; disciple; votary, worshipper.
s.f. The ceremony of waving over the heads of a bride and bridegroom an imitation lamp made of flour, and also some water, which is then thrown on either side of them.
more: n. f. The ceremony of waving over the heads of the bride and bridegroom an imitation lamp made of flour, and also some water which is then thrown on either side of them ( parachhnā ).

def: s.m. The aquatic plant Vallisneria octandra (see siwār).
s.m. The act of following; serving, service ( = sevā); devotion (to), addiction (to); using, use (syn. istěʻmāl), practising, practice
more: n. m. See sevā . Use; service. Is aukhad kā sevan karo .
subs: (-kā), To serve; to be devoted or addicted (to); to make use (of), to practise.

def: part. adj. To be served, requiring or deserving service or attendance; worshipful; — s.m. A master, &c.

s.m. = siwār, q.v. s.m. (f.?) The aquatic plant Vallisneria octandra, or Amaranthus sanguinus; the green moss-like substance which grows on the surface of stagnant water, or at the bottom and sides of ponds and tanks; vegetable mould, moss.
s.f. Sugar that has been purified with sewār or siwār, q.v.
more: n. f. Sugar that has been purified with a grass called sivār; Valisneria octandra , Wat.
s. f. Sugar that has been purified with سوار siwār, q. v.

def: adj. Tended, well-cared for, in good condition, plump, fat.
s.m. (f.? — dialec.), End, extremity; summit; termination, conclusion, close.
adj. (dialec.), Extreme, last; conclusive, closing, final, definitive.
s.f. A white rose; the dog-rose; China rose; Rosa glandulifera (syn. gul-ě-nasrīn).
more: n. f. S. सेमन्ति. The dog-rose; China rose, Rosa glandulifera .
s. f. A white rose. (Rosa glandulifera. Roxb. ).

def: adj. & s.m. = sevak, q.v. adj. & s.m. (f. -ikā), Serving; servile; dependent; — a servant, attendant; a worshipper, a votary; a disciple; follower; dependent; a loyal person, subject
subs: s.m. A deed or bond by which a person binds himself to servitude (either for a term or for life).

def: s.f. A female servant; disciple; votary, worshipper.
s.f. (dialec.) = siwaiyāṅ, q.v. s.f. Indian vermicelli (syn. semiyāṅ, simaiyāṅ; sewaʼīṅ, siwaʼiṅ, samaʼī, simaʼi)
subs: or siwaiyāṅ toṛnā, To make vermicelli.

def: part. Served, attended, waited on, honoured, worshipped; frequented.
n. f. 1. See سيوا (1). 2. Pupilage.

ety: fr. S. सेव˚, rt. सिव् 'to unite'
def: s.m. Name of a caste whose business it is to extract the juice of the date or palm (tāṛ) trees (so named from their climbing with a sīwan or loop passed round their feet).
more: (Beng. from سينا To stitch) s. m. Name of a cast, whose business it is to draw juice from the date or tāṛ trees, &c.

ety: sew˚ = Prk. सेव(इ) = S. सेव(ते), rt. सेव्
def: v.t. To attend on, serve; to adore, worship; to heed, give attention to; to protect, watch over, guard; to brood, incubate, rear, hatch, sit on (eggs)
more: v. a. 1. To attend on, to serve. 2. To brood, to rear, to sit, to incubate, to hatch.
subs: To sit on eggs, to brood; (fig.) to mope.

def: s.m. = سيوڙا sewṛā, q.v. s.m. Half-baked earthenware.
s.m. A faqīr of the Jain sect; — a conjurer.
more: s. m. A kind of faḳīr among the Hindūs. See سيورا sewrā.

def: s.m. Half-baked earthenware.
more: n. m. 1. A Jain faqīr . 2. A conjuror.
s. m. A kind of faḳīr: see H. سيوڙا sewṛā.

def: s.m. Any faqīr of the Jain sect.
s.f. Name of a female worshipper of Rāmćandra.
adj. = شيويه śaivya, q.v.
part. adj. To be served, deserving or requiring to be served, or worshipped, or obeyed, &c.; honourable, worshipful; estimable, respectable.

ety: Pārsī saw; old P. khsapa; Pehl. shap; Zend khshap; S. क्षप्
def: s.f. Night
more: n. f. Night ( rāt ). shabā-shab ; H. rātoṅ-rāt , adv. All night. shabāna-roz ; H. rāt din , Night and day. shab-bāsh , n. m. A night lodger. shab-bāshī , n. f. A night's halt or lodging. shab bakhair , intj. May the night pass well!

s. f. Night. (Pers. plur. شبہا or شبان). شب باز shab-bāz, s. A person who exhibits various figures for amusement at night, a puppet-show man: one who is up or vigilant at night: a bat. شب باش shab-bāsh, One who stays all night, a lodger. شب باشي shab-bāsh-ī, s. f. Staying all night, nightly lodging. شب بخير shab ba-ḵẖair, Good night, salutation at night. شب برات shab-i-barāt, s. f. The 14th day of the month Shaﻌbān, on which the Musalmāns make offerings and oblations, in the names (if not to the manes) of deceased relations: see برات barāt. شب بيدار shab-bedār, Waking, or watching all night, sleepless. شب پره shab-para, s. m. A bird of night (as, a bat, owl, &c.). شب تار يا تاريک shab-i-tār, or -tārīk, A dark night. شب خوابي shab-ḵẖẉāb-ī, s. f. Night-clothes. شب خون shab-ḵẖūn, s. m. A night-attack. شب خوني shab-ḵẖūn-ī, s. f. Robbery at night. شب خيزي shab-ḵẖez-ī, Rising at night (to pray…
subs: adv. In the night-time, during the night; all night
s.m. A person who exhibits various figures for amusement at night, a puppet-showman; one who is up or vigilant at night; a bat
s.m. One who stays all night, a night lodger
s.f. Staying all night; a night's halt or lodging
May the night pass well, good night; salutation at night

s.f. The eve of the 14th day of the month Shaʻbān (on which a vigil is observed with prayers, feastings, illuminations, &c., and the Musalmāns make offerings and oblations in the names (if not to the manes) of deceased ancestors; on this night the lives and fortunes of mortals during the coming year are said to be registered in heaven)
(and gul-ě-shab-bo), s.m. (f.?) lit. 'Night-smelling,' the tuberose, Polianthes tuberosa
adj. Waking or watching all night; sleepless
or shab-parak, or shab-para, s.m. lit. 'Night-flier'; a bird of night (as a bat, an owl, &c.); see shappara
s.m. lit. 'Night-shining'; a fire-fly; a glow-worm; a gem, &c. that sparkles in the dark
s.m. lit. 'Night-lamp'; a carbuncle; a gem of the ruby kind; a sparkling or brilliant gem
s.m. 'The little night-lamp'; a fire-fly; a glow-worm
s.m. A night-attack; — shab-ḵẖūn mārnā (-par), To make a night-attack (on)
s.f. Robbery at night with bloodshed
adj. & s.m. Rising at night (to pray); pious, devout; — one who rises at night to pray
s.f. A dish of meat and vegetables (esp. turnips) dressed all night on the fire and eaten the next morning
adj. & s.m. Overtaken by night, benighted; — one who is benighted
adj. Dusky or dark-coloured, black (a horse); — s.m. Name of a horse of Siyāvush
s.m. One who walks or travels in the night; a night-watch; a thief
s.f. The night of the tenth of Moharram (on which Husain was slain)
s.f. 'The night of power,' the 27th of Ramazān (when the Qorʼān is said to have descended from heaven; — syn. lailatuʼl-qadr)
s.f. Night-blindness, nyctalopia (syn. rat-aundhā); — shab-gāh, s.f. Night-place to keep anything in; night-time
s.f. Going rounds by night, patrolling, watching at night
s.f. Nocturnal perambulation, patrolling; a procession at night
adj. Of the colour of night, dark as night, black

s.m. One who passes a sleepless night; a cricket; a nightingale; any animal that chirps or sings at night; — travelling after midnight and before sunrise; a servant that works or travels in the night-time; — early dawn; — attacking by night, night-march, nocturnal invasion
s.f. 'Night-moisture,' dew (syn. os); — s.m. A kind of fine linen, a very fine muslin
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian شب (šab). Cognate of Sanskrit क्षप् (kṣap).

ety: inf. n. of شبع 'to be satiated,' &c.
def: s.f. Fulness, satiety.
s. (dakh.) Night-attack: see شب خون shab-ḵẖūn, under شب shab.
more: s. (dakh.) for شبہہ shubha, q. v.

def: s.m. Jet; black or dark coral; agate; a bead.
more: n. m. شبه like ned. 1. Demur; suspense; question; scruple. 2. Uncertainty ( dubdhā ). 3. ( Law ) A legal defect; a flaw. shubah-i-khafī . Light presumption; slight suspicion. shubah-i-shadīd yā qavī , n. m. Strong presumption (of guilt) shubah-i-qatl , n. m. Homicide. shubah k. , v. a. To distrust; doubt; suspect. shubah-i-māqūl , n. m. Reasonable doubt. shubah miṭānā, yā nikālnā , v. a. To remove a doubt. shubah hai . Is doubtful. be-shubah, be-shak , adv. Undoubtedly; indisputably ( bilā-shak ).
s. m. f. An image, resemblance, similitude, likeness.

ety: inf. n. of شبّ 'to become a youth'
def: s.m. Youth, youthfulness, prime of manhood (supposed to be the state from sixteen to thirty-two).
more: n. m. شب Youth; the prime of life.
(from شبّ) s. m. Youth, prime of life.

def: s.m.f. A similar thing, a like, fellow, match, an analogue; an image, similitude, likeness, resemblance
wety: From Sanskrit शुभ (śubha).

def: n. f. Likeness; similitude; similarity.

ety: for A. شباهة, v.n. fr. شبه; see shabīh
def: s.f. Similarity, similitude, likeness, resemblance.
s.m. pl. (of shabaka), Nets, &c.
more: s. plur. of شبکه shabaka, q. v.

ety: for A. شبکة, v.n. fr. شبك 'to make reticulated,' &c.
def: s.m. A net; lattice; trellis; grating; a reticulated veil, &c. (syn. jāl, jālī).
more: (Heb. from شبک To weave) s. m. 1. A net. 2. A lattice, a reticulated veil.

ety: v.n. fr. تشعّب, 'to be separated,' v of شعب 'to separate'
def: s.m. The eighth month of the Arabian year (the 14th day of this month is the festival called barāt or shab-barāt; on this day an angel is supposed to examine a record on which is written the names of the living).
more: n. m. The 8th Arabian month. The 14th of this month is the festival of shab-i-barāt , q. v.

def: adj. & adv. Nocturnal; — by night
more: adv. By night. adj. Nocturnal. شبانه روز shabāna roz, The civil day of twenty four hours, night and day.
subs: s.m. & adv. Night and day, the civil day of twenty-four hours ( = Gr. νυχθημερον).

def: s.f. The evening; a lodging-place for the night; a fold (for cattle or sheep).
s.f. The evening; a lodging-place for the night; a fold (for cattle or sheep).
more: s. f. 1. The entrance of night, evening. 2. A place for cattle or sheep to remain in at night.

def: s.m. & adv. = shabāna-roz, q.v.
(same as شب وروز) adv. Night and day, i. e. always.
intj. corr. of shābāsh, q.v. intj. Well done! bravo! excellent!

ety: shād, q.v.+bāsh, imperat. of būdan, 'to be'
def: intj. Well done! bravo! excellent!
more: intj. Cor. of shādbāsh from shād happy, bāsh be. Bravo! well done! 1. Shābash! Mullāṅ! tere tāvīz ko bāṅdhte hī bachchā phaṛkā! Mah. Wom. Bravo! Mulla! your amulets I no sooner wore than I felt a quickening in my womb. 2. Sābas tere saūr ko survā pakā līyā! Sakkar ko ghol ghāl-ke sarbat banā līyā . (In ridicule of the illiterate who pronounce s for sh ). Well done! how clever! actually a soup you've made! You've mixed and stirred the sugar and a sharbat made! shābāsh'ī , n. f. Applause; plaudits. shābāshī d ., v. a. To praise; applaud.
(contract. of شادباش) interj. Bravo! excellent! hey! be glad!
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian شاباش (šābāš).

def: adv. In the night time, during the night.
s.f. Praise, applause, plaudits
more: (contract. of شادباشي) s. f. Praise, applause.

ety: act. part. of شبّ 'to become a youth,' &c.
def: adj. & s.m. Youthful, in the prime of manhood; — a young man, a youth.
more: (part. act. of شبّ To grow, come to maturity, burn, kindle) s. m. A youth.
